person.name: Jutta von Schwaben

memoryArtifacts.headline: Grave stone of Judith of Hohenstaufen

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Judith of Swabia, also known as Judith of Hohenstaufen, (c. 1133/1134 – 7 July 1191), a member of the Hohenstaufen dynasty, was Landgravine of Thuringia from 1150 until 1172 by her marriage with the Ludovingian landgrave Louis II. She was baptized as Judith, but was commonly called Jutta or Guta. Sometimes the Latinate form Clementia was used, or Claritia or Claricia.

Judith was a daughter of Duke Frederick II of Swabia (1090–1147) and his second wife Agnes of Saarbrücken, thereby a younger half-sister of Emperor Frederick Barbarossa (1122–1190). She first appeared in contemporary sources in 1150, upon her marriage with Landgrave Louis II of Thuringia. This wedlock was intended to cement the relationship between the Thuringian Ludovingians and the imperial House of Hohenstaufen, to strengthen Emperor Barbarossa in his fierce conflict with Duke Henry the Lion and the House of Welf.

When in 1168 her husband reconciled with Henry the Lion, Judith began the construction of Runneburg Castle in Weißensee. The neighboring Counts of Beichlingen objected and protested to Emperor Barbarossa. However, the emperor sided with his half-sister and rejected the protests. Runneburg Castle was situated halfway between Wartburg Castle and Neuenburg Castle and became the residence of the Landgraves of Thuringia. Later during the conflicts between Germany's most powerful dynasties, the strategically located Runneburg Castle became one of the most important castles in the area.

Judith survived both her husband and her eldest son Landgrave Louis III. She died on 7 July 1191 and was buried in Reinhardsbrunn monastery next to her husband.

Her name is still omnipresent in Weißensee, which shows how highly she was regarded during her lifetime.

Judith's grave stone was created in the 14th century, well after her death. It must have been installed after the fire of 1292. It was moved from Reinhardsbrunn to the choir of the St. George's Church in Eisenach.

The Landgravine is depicted holding a lap dog in her left arm, while her right hand holds a scepter. A wide cantilevered canopy, held up by two angels, is extended over her head. The angels appear to sit on a pillow behind her head. The inscription reads S. SOROR FRIDERICI INPERATORIS ("the sister of Emperor Frederick").

Due to the canopy, this gravestone was larger than those of the other Landgraves of Thuringia (which are also on display in the St. George church in Eisenach). It must have made her grave very visible, even when the gravestone was part of the church floor. The presence of the Emperor's sister in the family tree introduced additional honor, which is why her family background was emphasized in the inscription.

In 1150, Judith married Louis II, Landgrave of Thuringia. They had the following children:

Louis III (1151–1190), succeeded her husband as Landgrave of Thuringia
Herman I (d. 1217), succeeded his brother as Landgrave of Thuringia
Henry Raspe III (c. 1155 – 18 July 1217), Count of Gudensberg
Frederick (c. 1155 – 1229), Count of Ziegenhain
Judith, married Herman II, Count of Ravensberg

sourceList.title: Foundation for Medieval Genealogy - JUTTA [Claricia] von Staufen ([1135]-7 Jul 1191, bur Reinhardsbrunn).
Jutta Claricia von Hohenstaufen, "Find A Grave Index"

zh.lifeSummary: 當Jutta von Schwaben於1133年出生在德國貝也史瓦本時，她的父親Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben43歲，母親Agnes von Saarbrücken19歲。她於1150年與Ludwig II. der Eiserne von Thüringen結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1191年7月7日在德國賀林根藍德可萊斯 格塔福利德利可洛達萊音哈斯布盧恩享年58歲，埋葬在德國賀林根藍德可萊斯 格塔福利德利可洛達萊音哈斯布盧恩。

meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Schwaben
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Jutta
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Judith  , also used as an independent given name.

en.lifeSummary: When Jutta von Schwaben was born in 1133, in Swabia, Bavaria, Germany, her father, Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben, was 43 and her mother, Agnes von Saarbrücken, was 19. She married Ludwig II. der Eiserne von Thüringen in 1150. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died on 7 July 1191, in Reinhardsbrunn, Friedrichroda, Gotha, Thuringia, Germany, at the age of 58, and was buried in Reinhardsbrunn, Friedrichroda, Gotha, Thuringia, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jutta von Schwaben est née en 1133, à Schwaben, Bayern, Allemagne, son père, Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Agnes von Saarbrücken, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Ludwig II. der Eiserne von Thüringen en 1150. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée le 7 juillet 1191, à Reinhardsbrunn, Friedrichroda, Landkreis Gotha, Thüringen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterrée à Reinhardsbrunn, Friedrichroda, Landkreis Gotha, Thüringen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jutta von Schwaben 1133 in Schwaben, Bayern, Deutschland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben 43 und ihre Mutter Agnes von Saarbrücken 19. Sie hat 1150 Ludwig II. der Eiserne von Thüringen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb am 7. Juli 1191 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Reinhardsbrunn, Friedrichroda, Landkreis Gotha, Thüringen, Deutschland und wurde in Reinhardsbrunn, Friedrichroda, Landkreis Gotha, Thüringen, Deutschland begraben.

'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwabenは43歳，母Agnes von Saarbrückenは19歳であった。1150年にLudwig II. der Eiserne von Thüringenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1191年7月7日にドイツスuリンジェンランドクレイス ゴサフリードリチロダレインハードスブルンで58歳で死去し，ドイツスuリンジェンランドクレイス ゴサフリードリチロダレインハードスブルンにて埋葬された。

ko.Reinhardsbrunn, Friedrichroda, Gotha, Th: 독일 스u링 랜드크레이스 가스아 프리드리크로다 레인하르드스브루
ko.lifeSummary: Jutta von Schwaben(이)가 1133년 독일 베이른 슈와벤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben(은)는 43세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes von Saarbrücken(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1150년 Ludwig II. der Eiserne von Thüringen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1191년 7월 7일 독일 스u링 랜드크레이스 가스아 프리드리크로다 레인하르드스브루에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 스u링 랜드크레이스 가스아 프리드리크로다 레인하르드스브루에 묻혔다.

'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Agnes von Saarbrücken, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com Ludwig II. der Eiserne von Thüringen em 1150. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jutta von Schwaben родилась в 1133 г. в местности щвабен, беиирн, Германия, её отцу по имени Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes von Saarbrücken, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ludwig II. der Eiserne von Thüringen в 1150 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла 7 июля 1191 г. в местности ринардсбрунн, фриедричрода, ландкрис гота, тuринген, Германия в возрасте 58 лет и была похоронена в местности ринардсбрунн, фриедричрода, ландкрис гота, тuринген, Германия.

Murió el 7 de julio de 1191, en Reinhardsbrunn, Friedrichroda, Landkreis Gotha, Thüringen, Alemania, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultada en Reinhardsbrunn, Friedrichroda, Landkreis Gotha, Thüringen, Alemania.
